LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ADRIENNE GIOTTA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OR BOTH. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATIONS IS GRANTED AS TO THE INVOLUNTARY DISSOLUTION OF A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION UNDER CORP. CODE 1800(B)(5) AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTIES. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED ON THE GROUNDS OF DISPUTED ISSUES OF FACT WITH REGARD TO PERSISTENT ABUSE OF AUTHORITY AS TO THE INVOLUNTARY DISSOLUTION OF A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION UNDER CORP. CODE 1800(B)(4). THE COURT ALSO OVERRULED ON THE OBJECTION TO EVIDENCE BASED ON NON-TIMELY SUBMITTAL OF THE ACTUAL EVIDENCE.PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Scarpino, Esq. 415-516-6147 for Plaintiff.
Dennis Sullivan Kenny, Esq. 415-433-1099 of Scherer Smith & Kenny LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues potential conflict. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FABIO GIOTTA'S Motion To Disqualify Counsel.

Plaintiff Fabio Giotta's motion to disqualify the law firm of Scherer Smith & Kenny from representing both the corporate defendants and defendant Ida Zoubi in this case is denied. Mr. Giotta has not alleged a derivative claim, made allegations that are "essentially derivative in nature," or otherwise identified an actual conflict between the corporate defendants and Ms. Zoubi. (Coldren v. Hart, King & Coldren, Inc. (2015) 239 Cal. App. 4th 237). This motion is controlled by Coldren and must be denied for the same two reasons that Coldren reversed a trial court's grant of a motion to disqualify the law firm in that case: 1) Mr. Giotta lacks standing to challenge the alleged conflict and 2) no conflict has been shown. The fact that, unlike Coldren, there has been no Corporations Code 2000 buyout demand in this case does not make Coldren inapplicable to this case. The discussion in Coldren about the section 2000 demand was corroboratory to the holding that, absent a derivative claim or derivative-like allegation, there is no conflict precluding a law firm from representing both a controlling shareholder and the corporation. Gong v. RFG Oil, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal. App. 4th 209, relied on by Mr. Giotta, is not applicable, as stated in Coldren, because Mr. Giotta has not alleged either a derivative claim or made allegations that are "essentially derivative in nature." (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
